statement.p: A tablet is for sale in a state where sales tax applies. The sales tax rate is {5.8\%} and the total was {\$314}\text{.} What was the price before sales tax? Set up an equation to answer this question. There is no need to solve it.

solution.p: This is a percent exercise, and we can use

In this case, the initial value is the cost before the sales tax is applied, and this is the thing we do not yet know. We decide to use p to represent this quantity, standing for price. The sales tax makes the final cost increase, so we use addition (not subtraction). The decimal for the percent of increase is 0.058\text{.} And the final value is the total, which is 314\text{.} So the equation to set up is:
